message.content:
POLARIS MISSION: beat ChatGPT + Gemini + FS-Researcher on deep-research reports.
  DEPTH = process ALL 346 baskets. FAITHFULNESS = context NLI + STRICT numbers.
BINDING INVARIANTS (docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md): faithfulness engine (strict_verify + NLI +
numeric + provenance) is the ONLY hard gate; external content re-enters ONLY via the fold-in seam;
computed numbers render ONLY via the verified [#calc:] lane (never [CITE:ev_xxx]); exploratory
execute_python is BARRED from rendering; weight-and-consolidate, never filter.
FENCE: work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix (branch bot/compose-fix). NEVER touch another worktree or the
operator's /home/polaris/polaris_project monitoring dir. Report REAL output only; never fake a pass.


YOU ARE THE BUILDER (Opus hands) for "compose". Fable already found the problems and
told you EXACTLY what to change. DO NOT re-investigate, DO NOT hunt for an entrypoint, DO NOT wander.
Apply ONLY these fixes to launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh, in order:
[
  {
    "severity": "P0",
    "file_line": "launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh:35-43 (add export) / src/polaris_graph/llm/judge_concurrency.py:49,55",
    "problem": "Every NLI judge POST is gated by a process-global threading.BoundedSemaphore sized by PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY — VERIFIED read at judge_concurrency.py:49/81-99, default DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY=4 at :55, acquired around every POST at entailment_judge.py:155 — and VERIFIED set in NO compose launcher and no run_s5_i3.py setdefault. Once fix #1 lands, all verify threads queue behind 4 slots; the 0615bc5 pre-pass speedup is itself capped at 4. The cap was tuned for a credibility-burst 429 storm, not compose; 0 429s at 128-way proves rate limit is not the ceiling. (Dedups 5 duplicate findings on judge_concurrency.py:55/:178.)",
    "fix": "Add `export PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY=16` to launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh AND `os.environ.setdefault(\"PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY\", \"16\")` in the run_s5_i3.py:342-359 setdefault block (launcher/env still wins). Pure config, zero code change. Optional follow-up: phase-aware override via the existing _CONCURRENCY_OVERRIDE mechanism (judge_concurrency.py:61) so the de-storm stays tight for the credibility burst.",
    "acceptance": "Startup log / one-line probe shows resolve_max_concurrency() == 16 (knob actually read); during compose, >4 judge POSTs observed concurrently in flight (in-flight counter or timestamp-overlap on judge POST start/end logs); 429 rate stays ~0."
  },
  {
    "severity": "P1",
    "file_line": "launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh:38 / src/polaris_graph/llm/entailment_judge.py:155,196-199",
    "problem": "A hung judge POST holds a semaphore slot for the full PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S=300s (launcher raised it from the 150s code default without raising the 4-slot pool), and PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES — VERIFIED read via _ENV_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES at entailment_judge.py:199, whose own comment at :196 says the run slate is supposed to set it to 1 — is set NOWHERE, defaulting to 2, so one sentence can burn 3x300s = 900s of slot time. (Dedups both slot-hold findings.)",
    "fix": "Add `export PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES=1` to launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh + setdefault in run_s5_i3.py (the value the code comment prescribes). Separately consider lowering PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S from 300 back toward 150 (measured p90 13-14s) as a follow-up measured against blank-200 recurrence, since Fix-6 raised it deliberately. Do not weaken fail-closed behavior.",
    "acceptance": "Probe shows resolved total-deadline retries == 1 (knob actually read); a forced-hang test shows a single sentence holds a judge slot for at most 2 x total_s before fail-closed drop, not 3x."
  }
]

For each: make the concrete change at the file:line; then ACTUALLY EXERCISE the touched path (run it,
py_compile is NOT enough — the acceptance observable tells you what to run); then git commit in
/home/polaris/wt/compose_fix with an honest message (say PARTIAL if partial). Append one dated line to
/home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md. Report what landed vs what Fable asked. A skipped fix reported is fine; hidden is not.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
On branch bot/compose-fix
nothing to commit, working tree clean
0615bc5 compose: unblock the event loop — NLI verify off-loop via asyncio.to_thread
eff82fb compose gear-loop iter 5: GEAR-RULE newest-cp4+cp3 auto-resolve launcher + 429 self-contention concurrency cut (builds on iter-4 resume)
4e0b1f9 compose gear-loop iter 4: section-level crash RESUME (SHA-gated) + self-relaunch launcher

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# Compose gear-loop iter 5 — builds on iter 4 (section-level crash RESUME + self-relaunch). Two changes,
3	# both faithfulness-NEUTRAL (numeric forward-match + context-level NLI entailment UNCHANGED; the lexical
4	# >=2-word overlap gate stays DELETED at this branch head; nothing question-tuned):
5	#   (A) GEAR RULE (operator 2026-07-10): iter-4 HARDCODED the pinned cp3/cp4 paths. iter-5 RESOLVES the
6	#       NEWEST outline cp4 AND newest corpus cp3 at launch (ls -t) so the compose gear auto-picks up new
7	#       S3/S4 rounds as they land — the gears turn into each other. The pinned s4_gear/s3_gear ARE the
8	#       newest today, so pin + newest coincide; the dynamic resolve keeps that true on the NEXT S3/S4 round
9	#       without a launcher edit. Both resolved paths are PRINTED for the round report.
10	#   (B) 429 SELF-CONTENTION (observed in the iter-4 run 08:33-09:02: repeated OpenRouter 429 backoff +
11	#       a 3-min stall while a second heavy OpenRouter job ran in a sibling worktree). Drop the compose's
12	#       OpenRouter concurrency (PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM 30->16, writer 24->12) so the gear loop coexists with
13	#       sibling jobs on the shared OpenRouter rate limit with FEWER backoffs -> sections FINALIZE and reach
14	#       the iter-4 per-section checkpoint sooner (a kill then loses less work). max_tokens slate unchanged
15	#       (a CAP, not a target -> reasoning is never starved; §9.1.8).
16	set -a; . /workspace/POLARIS/.env 2>/dev/null; set +a
17	cd /workspace/compose_wt || exit 9
18	export PYTHONPATH=/workspace/compose_wt PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8
19	# -- activation flags (ghost-free slate, iter-4 identical) --
20	export PG_COMPOSE_NO_RAW_SPAN_FALLBACK=1
21	export PG_SECTION_BASKET_MAP=1
22	export PG_SYNTH_PRIMARY=1
23	export PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER=1
24	export PG_VERIFIED_COMPOSE=1
25	export PG_VERIFIED_COMPOSE_MULTICITED=1
26	export PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=enforce
27	export PG_WRITER_DEADLINE_TRANSPORT_AWARE=1
28	export PG_CROSS_SECTION_REPETITION_GUARD=1
29	export PG_RENDER_SEAM_SANITIZE=1
30	# -- B4 resilience levers + throughput (iter-4 proven) --
31	export PG_WRITER_WALL_BASKET_SCALED=1
32	export PG_WRITER_KSPAN_RECOVERY_PASS=1
33	# iter-5 (B): lower concurrency to cut 429 self-contention with sibling OpenRouter jobs.
34	export PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY=12
35	export PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=16
36	export PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CALL_DEADLINE_S=300
37	export PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_WALL_DEADLINE_S=1400
38	export PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S=300
39	# -- §9.1.8 always-MAX token slate (z-ai/glm-5.2 real caps) --
40	export PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_MAX_TOKENS=131072
41	export PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS=65536
42	export PG_S5_SPAN_CHAR_CAP=8000
43	export PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=2
44	# -- iter-5 (A): PINNED best inputs are the DEFAULT; the GEAR RULE resolves the NEWEST cp4+cp3 and uses
45	#    them when they are newer than the pin. Today pin == newest, so both resolve to s4_gear/s3_gear. --
46	CP2=/workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s2_hamster_i1/cp2_corpus_snapshot.json
47	PIN_CP3=/workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s3_gear/cp3_basket_snapshot.json
48	PIN_CP4=/workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s4_gear/cp4_outline_snapshot.json
49	NEW_CP4=$(ls -t /workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s4_*/cp4_outline_snapshot.json 2>/dev/null | head -1)
50	NEW_CP3=$(ls -t /workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s3_*/cp3_basket_snapshot.json 2>/dev/null | head -1)
51	CP4=${NEW_CP4:-$PIN_CP4}
52	CP3=${NEW_CP3:-$PIN_CP3}
53	OUT=/workspace/compose_wt/outputs/s5_gear_iter5
54	CKPT=$OUT/ckpt
55	LOG=$OUT/compose.log
56	mkdir -p "$CKPT"
57	# -- FAIL-LOUD ghost-free pre-flight (operator P0 wiring lock) --
58	GHOST=$(grep -c writer_numeric_dropped src/polaris_graph/generator/abstractive_writer.py || true)
59	if [ "$GHOST" != "0" ]; then
60	  echo "[ABORT] ghost present: writer_numeric_dropped count=$GHOST" | tee -a "$LOG"; exit 3
61	fi
62	if ! python -u -c "from src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator import _synth_primary_enabled; import sys; sys.exit(0 if _synth_primary_enabled() else 7)"; then
63	  echo "[ABORT] _synth_primary_enabled() is False under this env slate" | tee -a "$LOG"; exit 4
64	fi
65	echo "[preflight] ghost_absent(writer_numeric_dropped=0) AND synth_primary_enabled=True -> OK" | tee -a "$LOG"
66	{
67	  echo "=== iter-5 launch $(date -u) ==="
68	  echo "CP2=$CP2"; echo "CP3(resolved)=$CP3"; echo "CP4(resolved)=$CP4"
69	  echo "pin_cp3=$PIN_CP3 newest_cp3=$NEW_CP3"; echo "pin_cp4=$PIN_CP4 newest_cp4=$NEW_CP4"
70	  echo "commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD)"
71	  echo "PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=$PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY=$PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY"
72	} | tee -a "$LOG"
73	for attempt in $(seq 1 8); do
74	  [ -f "$OUT/cp5_generation_snapshot.json" ] && { echo "[loop] cp5 present -> DONE" | tee -a "$LOG"; break; }
75	  echo "=== [loop] attempt $attempt START $(date -u) ===" | tee -a "$LOG"
76	  python -u scripts/run_s5_i3.py     --cp2 "$CP2" --cp3 "$CP3" --cp4 "$CP4"     --out "$OUT/cp5_generation_snapshot.json"     --ckpt-dir "$CKPT"     --cap-primary 0 < /dev/null >> "$LOG" 2>&1
77	  echo "=== [loop] attempt $attempt EXIT rc=$? $(date -u) ===" | tee -a "$LOG"
78	  [ -f "$OUT/cp5_generation_snapshot.json" ] && { echo "[loop] cp5 written -> DONE" | tee -a "$LOG"; break; }
79	  sleep 5
80	done
81	echo "=== iter-5 loop finished $(date -u) cp5_exists=$([ -f "$OUT/cp5_generation_snapshot.json" ] && echo yes || echo no) ===" | tee -a "$LOG"
82

content.text: Now let me read the two source files referenced.

content.content:
1	"""I-deepfix-001 (de-storm) — bounded, env-driven CONCURRENCY cap for the per-claim mirror-chain
2	side judges (``entailment_judge`` + ``credibility_judge_caller`` + ``semantic_conflict_detector``).
3	
4	ROOT CAUSE this cures (forensically observed on the 2026-07-04 super-serious deepener preflight,
5	``/root/deepener_preflight.log``): each of the three side judges fires a BLOCKING glm-5.2 mirror-chain
6	POST per claim, driven from a BOUNDED-PARALLEL verify / tiering / consolidation pool. But NOTHING
7	capped how many of those POSTs were in flight AT ONCE. Under the verify burst ~32 entailment POSTs
8	hit the mirror-chain LEAD host (``friendli``) at essentially the same instant — an account-QPS 429
9	storm on ONE host ("429 Too Many Requests" x32). The chain alternates (``novita`` / ``z-ai``) were
10	DNS-degraded on the box at the time ("Temporary failure in name resolution"), so provider rotation
11	kept landing back on ``friendli``; the per-claim judge then burned its retry budget on ~15-50s
12	rate-limit backoffs and, on exhaustion, emitted the fail-CLOSED ``('ENTAILED','judge_error: ...')``
13	sentinel. Consumers DROP that sentinel, so the entailment-requiring layers (the abstract / the
14	full-verify pass) collapsed to empty while the per-section body — verified earlier in smaller,
15	time-spread bursts — kept its claims.
16	
17	The CURE (transport-only, faithfulness-NEUTRAL): cap the number of side-judge provider POSTs that are
18	IN FLIGHT at once with a process-global bounded semaphore, SHARED across all three judges so the cap
19	is on TOTAL glm-5.2 mirror QPS (they all hit the SAME ``[friendli, novita, z-ai, phala]`` chain).
20	Fewer simultaneous POSTs per host -> fewer 429s -> fewer retry-exhaustion fail-closed drops -> MORE
21	claims verified with the SAME verdicts (a §-1.3 STRENGTHENING — it never relaxes faithfulness). This
22	composes with the existing ``judge_burst_spread`` round-robin START-host spread: with ``N`` in flight
23	spread over 4 hosts each host sees ~``N/4`` normally, and ``N`` is the worst-case ceiling if the
24	alternates are unreachable and every call rotates back to the lead.
25	
26	It changes ONLY *when* a POST is admitted — never the model, prompt, temperature, ``max_tokens``,
27	reasoning effort, JSON verdict parsing, provider rotation, or the fail-closed sentinel. A timed-out /
28	errored member still fails CLOSED exactly as before; this helper can neither manufacture nor flip a
29	verdict.
30	
31	LAW VI — env-driven (single knob, ``PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY``):
32	  * unset                       -> ``DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY`` (the de-storm is ON by default).
33	  * ``N`` (>= 1)                -> at most ``N`` side-judge POSTs in flight at once.
34	  * ``0``                       -> UNBOUNDED escape hatch: ``acquire_judge_slot()`` is a no-op
35	                                   context manager, byte-identical to the pre-fix behavior.
36	  * negative / malformed        -> ``DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY`` (a typo must not silently disable the
37	                                   storm protection — the safe direction).
38	
39	The bound is resolved at each ``acquire`` (so a test / bakeoff can re-point the env within one
40	process) and the process-global semaphore is (re)built lazily whenever the resolved bound changes,
41	guarded by a lock. Stdlib-only leaf module -> zero off-mode import cost.
42	"""
43	from __future__ import annotations
44	
45	import contextlib
46	import os
47	import threading
48	
49	ENV_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY = "PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY"
50	
51	# 4 mirror-chain hosts ([friendli, novita, z-ai, phala]); with burst-spread this is ~1 POST/host
52	# normally and a worst-case ceiling of 4 on the lead if the alternates are unreachable — comfortably
53	# below the ~32-simultaneous burst that 429-stormed friendli. Operators can raise this via the env if
54	# the provider capacity allows (the memory standard is "<= 4 agents in flight").
55	DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY = 4
56	
57	_LOCK = threading.Lock()
58	_SEMAPHORE: threading.BoundedSemaphore | None = None
59	_SEMAPHORE_BOUND: int | None = None
60	
61	# I-deepfix-001 (box2 credibility-pass SPEED fix): a PROCESS-GLOBAL, thread-safe PHASE OVERRIDE. When
62	# set (> 0) it REPLACES the env-resolved cap for the DURATION of a wrapped phase — used to raise the
63	# side-judge concurrency ONLY while the advisory credibility pass runs (both its legs: the credibility
64	# scorer AND the basket-member entailment verify go through ``acquire_judge_slot``), leaving the
65	# composition-time entailment cap at its protected env value. Default None => no override => the env
66	# path below is byte-identical. Set/cleared by ``credibility_pass_concurrency`` around the pass; on the
67	# one-query-per-VM model no two passes overlap in a process.
68	_OVERRIDE_LOCK = threading.Lock()
69	_CONCURRENCY_OVERRIDE: int | None = None
70	
71	
72	class JudgeSlotTimeout(RuntimeError):
73	    """Raised by ``acquire_judge_slot(timeout=…)`` when a slot is not admitted within the deadline.
74	
75	    A wedged slot-holder (e.g. the documented GIL/fut.result stall) can no longer freeze the run: an
76	    ADVISORY caller (the credibility judge) catches this and degrades THAT row to a disclosed
77	    ``judge_error`` priors fallback. Binding callers that pass no timeout are unaffected (default
78	    ``None`` == the pre-fix unbounded acquire)."""
79	
80	
81	def resolve_max_concurrency() -> int:
82	    """Resolve the configured in-flight side-judge cap.
83	
84	    Precedence: an active ``credibility_pass_concurrency`` PHASE OVERRIDE (> 0) wins; otherwise the
85	    env ``PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY``. Returns the cap ``N >= 1`` when bounded, or ``0`` for
86	    UNBOUNDED (the explicit ``"0"`` escape hatch). Unset -> ``DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY``. A negative or
87	    malformed value -> ``DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY`` (a typo must not silently disable the de-storm).
88	    Never raises (a transport-config read must not break the judge)."""
89	    with _OVERRIDE_LOCK:
90	        override = _CONCURRENCY_OVERRIDE
91	    if override is not None and override > 0:
92	        return override
93	    raw = os.environ.get(ENV_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY, "").strip()
94	    if raw == "":
95	        return DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY
96	    try:
97	        n = int(raw)
98	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
99	        return DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY
100	    if n == 0:
101	        return 0  # explicit unbounded escape hatch (byte-identical to the pre-fix behavior)
102	    if n < 0:
103	        return DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY
104	    return n
105	
106	
107	@contextlib.contextmanager
108	def credibility_pass_concurrency(bound: int | None):
109	    """Temporarily REPLACE the side-judge concurrency cap for the wrapped block (the credibility pass).
110	
111	    ``bound`` <= 0 / None => no override (byte-identical: the env cap governs). ``bound`` >= 1 raises
112	    the effective cap to exactly ``bound`` for the DURATION of the block — every ``acquire_judge_slot``
113	    call on any thread reads it via ``resolve_max_concurrency``. Restores the prior value on EVERY exit
114	    (success or exception). Faithfulness-neutral: it changes only HOW MANY advisory/entailment POSTs are
115	    admitted at once during this ONE phase; it never touches the model, prompt, verdict, or gate. Leaves
116	    the composition-time side-judge cap (the env value) untouched, so the storm protection there holds.
117	    The (re)built semaphore is swapped atomically per ``_get_semaphore``; in-flight holders release the
118	    object they acquired, so a mid-flight bound change never corrupts a live acquire."""
119	    global _CONCURRENCY_OVERRIDE
120	    try:
121	        n = int(bound) if bound is not None else 0
122	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
123	        n = 0
124	    if n <= 0:
125	        yield
126	        return
127	    with _OVERRIDE_LOCK:
128	        prev = _CONCURRENCY_OVERRIDE
129	        _CONCURRENCY_OVERRIDE = n
130	    try:
131	        yield
132	    finally:
133	        with _OVERRIDE_LOCK:
134	            _CONCURRENCY_OVERRIDE = prev
135	
136	
137	def _get_semaphore(bound: int) -> threading.BoundedSemaphore:
138	    """Return the process-global ``BoundedSemaphore`` for ``bound`` (>= 1), rebuilding it iff the
139	    resolved bound changed since the last call. In production the bound is constant so the semaphore
140	    is built exactly once; a test that re-points the env gets a fresh semaphore. In-flight holders
141	    release the object they acquired, so a rebuild never corrupts a live acquire."""
142	    global _SEMAPHORE, _SEMAPHORE_BOUND
143	    with _LOCK:
144	        if _SEMAPHORE is None or _SEMAPHORE_BOUND != bound:
145	            _SEMAPHORE = threading.BoundedSemaphore(bound)
146	            _SEMAPHORE_BOUND = bound
147	        return _SEMAPHORE
148	
149	
150	@contextlib.contextmanager
151	def acquire_judge_slot(timeout: float | None = None):
152	    """Admit at most ``resolve_max_concurrency()`` side-judge provider POSTs concurrently.
153	
154	    Wrap the blocking provider POST in ``with acquire_judge_slot():``. When the cap is ``0``
155	    (unbounded escape hatch) this is a no-op — byte-identical to the pre-fix path. Otherwise it
156	    acquires one slot of the shared process-global bounded semaphore for the duration of the POST and
157	    releases it on EVERY exit path (success, timeout, or exception), so a hung/timed-out call frees
158	    its slot when its total-deadline force-closes it. TRANSPORT-ONLY: it never touches the verdict.
159	
160	    ``timeout`` (I-deepfix-001 box2 fix): when a positive deadline is supplied and no slot is admitted
161	    within it, raise ``JudgeSlotTimeout`` BEFORE the POST (nothing acquired => nothing released). This
162	    is the graceful-degrade path for the ADVISORY credibility judge: a wedged slot-holder can no longer
163	    strangle the run — the timed-out row falls back to a disclosed ``judge_error`` prior. ``None`` (the
164	    default, used by every binding caller) keeps the pre-fix UNBOUNDED acquire, byte-identical."""
165	    bound = resolve_max_concurrency()
166	    if bound <= 0:
167	        yield
168	        return
169	    sem = _get_semaphore(bound)
170	    if timeout is not None and timeout > 0:
171	        if not sem.acquire(timeout=timeout):
172	            raise JudgeSlotTimeout(
173	                f"side-judge slot not admitted within {timeout:g}s (cap={bound}) — degrading this "
174	                "advisory judge call to a disclosed judge_error prior so a wedged slot-holder can "
175	                "never freeze the run (transport-only; no verdict/gate touched)"
176	            )
177	    else:
178	        sem.acquire()
179	    try:
180	        yield
181	    finally:
182	        sem.release()
183	
184	
185	def _reset_for_test() -> None:
186	    """TEST-ONLY: drop the process-global semaphore so the next ``acquire`` rebuilds it from the
187	    current env. Not called on any production path."""
188	    global _SEMAPHORE, _SEMAPHORE_BOUND
189	    with _LOCK:
190	        _SEMAPHORE = None
191	        _SEMAPHORE_BOUND = None
192

330	    # errors) reach the tee'd compose.log — the driver previously never called basicConfig, so only
331	    # WARNING+ was visible and the writer-yield collapse was undiagnosable. Also install the marker tally
332	    # so the counts land in the cp5 payload.
333	    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(name)s %(message)s")
334	    logging.getLogger().setLevel(logging.INFO)
335	    marker_tally = _MarkerTally()
336	    logging.getLogger().addHandler(marker_tally)
337	
338	    # Fix 3 (2026-07-10 compose gear-loop): writer THROUGHPUT/RESILIENCE flags ON by default for this
339	    # driver so a transient transport disconnect never permanently kills a basket wave (the prior run
340	    # lost 3/5 sections' whole writer wave in 0-2s). setdefault => an explicit launcher/env value still
341	    # wins. General resilience knobs, not question-tuned; recorded in flag_slate for disclosure.
342	    for _k, _v in (
343	        ("PG_WRITER_DEADLINE_TRANSPORT_AWARE", "1"),
344	        ("PG_WRITER_WALL_BASKET_SCALED", "1"),
345	        ("PG_WRITER_KSPAN_RECOVERY_PASS", "1"),
346	    ):
347	        os.environ.setdefault(_k, _v)
348	
349	    # Fix 6 (2026-07-10 compose gear-loop): entailment-judge RELIABILITY for compose runs. The prior run

351	    # default max_tokens is already the provider chain MIN 131072 and effort "high", both the model max)
352	    # and "total_deadline_exceeded_150s". Route around blank-200 windows (PG_JUDGE_PROVIDER_ROTATE) and
353	    # grant a bounded larger total wall. Do NOT override PG_ENTAILMENT_MAX_TOKENS / _REASONING_EFFORT
354	    # (override clamps down / xhigh starves GLM per the mirror bake-off). setdefault => launcher/env wins.
355	    for _k, _v in (
356	        ("PG_JUDGE_PROVIDER_ROTATE", "1"),
357	        ("PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S", "300"),
358	    ):
359	        os.environ.setdefault(_k, _v)
360	
361	    sel = set()
362	    if args.sections.strip():
363	        sel = {int(x) for x in args.sections.split(",") if x.strip() != ""}
364	    ckpt_dir = Path(args.ckpt_dir) if args.ckpt_dir.strip() else None
365	    if ckpt_dir:
366	        ckpt_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
367

369	        SectionPlan,
370	        _run_section,

content.content:
185	# for a single judge call. A transient transport/parse/empty-choices fault on the entailment judge
186	# previously fell straight through to the fail-open ('ENTAILED','judge_error:…') sentinel, which the
187	# consumers DROP in enforce mode — an OVER-DROP of a salvageable verdict (the drb_72-class coverage
188	# collapse). Retry the POST+parse this many extra times before emitting the sentinel; default 2
189	# (=> up to 3 total attempts). 0 disables the retry (single attempt, the pre-#1191 behavior).
190	_DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_RETRIES = 2
191	# I-arch-007 A2 (entailment-speed fix): a total-deadline (trickle-hang) timeout re-hangs on the SAME
192	# pinned mirror provider, so the extra same-provider retries are ~_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S of dead wait that
193	# almost never recovers — the dominant contributor to the hang-path worst case (3x150s=450s). Cap a
194	# total_deadline_exceeded retry TIGHTER than the general transient-fault budget. DEFAULT here PRESERVES
195	# current behaviour (= the general retry count, so the OFF path is byte-identical, LAW VI); the run slate
196	# sets PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES=1 to apply the fix (=> up to 2 attempts on a hang vs 3 on a
197	# transient fault). Faithfulness-NEUTRAL: the verdict emitted on exhaustion is the SAME fail-closed
198	# ('ENTAILED','judge_error:…') sentinel the consumers DROP — fewer wasted retries, never a different verdict.
199	_ENV_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES = "PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES"
200	_DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES = _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_RETRIES
201	# Short fixed backoff between judge retries (seconds). Env-overridable per LAW VI.
202	_DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_RETRY_BACKOFF_S = 0.5
203	# U16 (I-deepfix-001): rate-limit / connectivity hardening. A real HTTP 429/503/502/504, a DNS
204	# "name resolution" ConnectError, or an OpenRouter 200-wrapped 429 error-envelope previously got the
205	# SAME tiny fixed backoff (0.5s) + tiny retry budget (2) as a bad-verdict, so a transient account-QPS
206	# 429 storm / DNS blip exhausted the ~3 attempts in ~1s and the claim fail-closed DROPPED — over ~178
207	# claims/report x bounded-parallel verify workers this self-sustained the storm, tore the seam, and
208	# blocked drb_75 checkpoint-resume. Fix (transport-only, faithfulness-NEUTRAL): a recoverable rate-
209	# limit/connectivity fault gets (a) a LARGER bounded retry budget and (b) exponential backoff with
210	# jitter honoring a rate-limit FLOOR + the server Retry-After header (mirrors openrouter_client.py's
211	# 429 floor/backoff, the AWS exp-backoff-and-jitter pattern already in fetch_limiter/llm_throttle, and
212	# OpenRouter's 2026 rate-limit guidance). The judge MODEL, the two-family/model lock, verdict parsing,
213	# provider rotation (iwire008 #1322 host-capacity recovery — PRESERVED), and the fail-CLOSED
214	# ('ENTAILED','judge_error:…') sentinel are ALL byte-unchanged; only the retry cadence recovers a REAL

export PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY=16
# iter-5 (Fable P1): a hung judge POST holds a semaphore slot for the full PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S. The
# code comment (entailment_judge.py:196) prescribes the run slate set this to 1 (=> up to 2 attempts on
# a hang vs the default 2 => 3x total_s of dead slot-hold). Faithfulness-NEUTRAL (same fail-closed sentinel).
export PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES=1
